prompt:
Construct a ShelterOps Desk Console supporting offline shelter operations across inventory, kennel availability, and compliance-grade reporting for a mid-size animal welfare organization. The application targets Windows 11 with an English UI and is designed for 1920x1080 and above with proper high-DPI scaling. Core roles include Administrator (system configuration, security policies), Operations Manager (dashboards, scheduling, approvals), Inventory Clerk (item receiving/issuance, barcode lookup), and Auditor (read-only access to logs and masked data). Staff work in multiple parallel windows: an "Intake & Kennel Board" for availability and bookability checks, an "Item Ledger" for supplies, and a "Reports Studio" for analytics and scheduled outputs; right-click context menus allow quick actions like "Issue 3 units," "Mark as expired," or "Open related outbound record." Keyboard-first workflows are mandatory (e.g., Ctrl+F global search, Ctrl+N new record, F2 edit, Ctrl+Shift+E export), with deep clipboard support for copying table rows as TSV for pasting into Excel. A system tray mode keeps the console running for long shifts, showing badge counts for low-stock and upcoming expirations, and allowing one-click open of alerts without relying on push services. Intelligent Search & Matching is expressed as an offline placement/recommendation tool: users filter/sort kennels and adoptable listings by area zone (e.g., Building A/Row 3), distance within the facility (feet between zones), pet type and restrictions, nightly price (USD) for boarding, available date ranges, and ratings; the system performs constraint-based bookability checks (no overlapping stays, capacity limits, restriction conflicts) and produces a ranked list with explainable reasons (e.g., "Meets 'no cats' restriction; available 03/27/2026 2:00 PM–04/02/2026 11:00 AM; within 150 ft of exam room").

Data persists locally in SQLite with a schema that includes household item ledger fields (category, storage location, quantity, unit cost, expiration date, optional serial number), inbound/outbound records with immutable timestamps, and alert thresholds (low-stock default: under 7 days of average usage; expiration alert: 14 days prior). Barcode entry and lookup must work offline via USB barcode scanners acting as keyboard input; invalid or duplicate serial numbers are rejected with a clear error and an audit entry. Reporting dashboards provide occupancy rate, bed/kennel turnover, maintenance response time, and overdue-fee distribution with multi-dimensional filtering (date range, location zone, pet type, staff owner) and exports to CSV/PDF; scheduled report generation runs via a local job scheduler with dependency orchestration (collect → cleanse → analyze → visualize), failure/anomaly alerting surfaced in-app (no email/SMS), and historical version comparison showing metric deltas between runs. Back-office operations include admin-managed product catalogs, inventory policies, after-sales adjustments, and marketing configuration (offline price rules and promos), with role-based permissions and a local job queue that throttles heavy exports to avoid UI freezes. Security/compliance requirements include encrypted local storage for sensitive fields (AES-256), encryption in transit for any optional LAN file-share sync (TLS with pinned certificates), key management stored in the OS credential vault where available, field-level masking for PII (e.g., show last 4 of phone numbers), consent flags, and retention controls (default delete or anonymize records after 7 years, configurable). End-to-end operation logs are append-only, searchable, and exportable for audits; API-style rate limiting and anti-bot controls apply to any local automation endpoints/plugins, with extensible device fingerprinting based on machine ID and operator session. The console is implemented in C++ with Dear ImGui for responsive desktop UI and SQLite for embedded persistence, includes crash checkpointing to restore the last open tabs and unsaved form states, provides offline update via importing a signed .msi package with rollback to the previous version, and enforces long-running stability through explicit resource cleanup, bounded caches, and leak detection in debug builds.
